sect1.title: Introduction
sect1.para: The SelectionTemplate class, implemented in Cython, serves as a foundational base for defining and applying event selections within the AnalysisG framework. It provides robust mechanisms for interpreting data from ROOT files, managing event weights, and associating metadata. This class is designed to be inherited by specific selection implementations, offering common functionalities for data processing and filtering, with a strong emphasis on performance through its C++ backend.

sect1.title: The SelectionTemplate Class
sect1.para: SelectionTemplate is a cdef class, indicating its implementation as a Cython extension type. This allows for direct and optimized interaction with C++ objects and types, which is crucial for efficient processing of large datasets in high-energy physics. It encapsulates a pointer (self.ptr) to a C++ selection_template object, managing its lifecycle and exposing its parameters and functionalities as Python properties and methods.

listitem.para: __cinit__(self): The Cython "constructor" equivalent, called during object allocation. If the instance is not SelectionTemplate itself (i.e., it's a subclass), it returns immediately. Otherwise, it initializes self.root_leaves as an empty dictionary and a new C++ selection_template object, assigning its pointer to self.ptr.

__init__(self, inpt = None): The Python initializer. If inpt (a dictionary) is provided, it attempts to set various attributes of self from inpt["data"]. It also initializes self.ptr.passed_weights and self.ptr.matched_meta from inpt["weights"] and inpt["meta"] respectively, converting them from Python types to C++ maps.

__dealloc__(self): The Cython "destructor" equivalent, called upon object deallocation. If the instance is not SelectionTemplate itself, it returns. Otherwise, it deallocates the C++ selection_template object pointed to by self.ptr, ensuring proper memory management.
__hash__(self): Computes a hash value for the selection object. It converts the first 8 characters of the C++ object's hash string to an integer.
__name__(self): Returns the name of the underlying C++ selection object as a Python string, using the env utility function for conversion.

__reduce__(self): Implements the __reduce__ protocol for pickling SelectionTemplate objects, allowing them to be serialized and deserialized. It captures non-callable attributes, passed_weights, and matched_meta.

cdef void transform_dict_keys(self): An empty placeholder method. This is intended to be overridden by subclasses to perform any necessary transformations on dictionary keys, for example, to match specific naming conventions or data structures.

dump(self, str path = "./pkl-data", str name = ""): Saves the current SelectionTemplate object to a pickle file.
path
The directory path to save the file. Defaults to "./pkl-data".
name
The filename (without extension). Defaults to self.ptr.name.

load(self, str path = "./pkl-data", str name = ""): Loads a SelectionTemplate object from a pickle file.
path
The directory path to load from. Defaults to "./pkl-data".
name
The filename (without extension). Defaults to self.ptr.name.
The loaded SelectionTemplate object or None if loading fails.

HashToWeightFile(self, hash_): Converts a given hash (or list/dictionary of hashes) to a list of tuples, where each tuple contains a filename and its associated weight. This method uses the underlying C++ reverse_hash functionality.
hash_
A string, list of strings, or dictionary of strings representing hashes.
A list of (filename, weight) tuples.
Postprocessing(self): An empty placeholder method intended to be overridden by subclasses for any post-processing steps required after data interpretation and selection.

InterpretROOT(self, str path, str tree): The core method for interpreting and processing data from ROOT files based on defined root_leaves.
Configuration Check: Verifies that self.root_leaves (a dictionary mapping leaf names to functions) is properly set. If not, it prints an error and returns self.
IO Setup: Initializes an IO object (from AnalysisG.core.io) with the provided path and tree, and the specified root_leaves. Verbosity is set to False.
Data Iteration: Iterates through the IO object, processing each event.
Leaf Association: Dynamically associates leaf names from the ROOT file with the functions defined in self.root_leaves.
Function Application: Applies the user-defined functions (from self.root_leaves.values()) to the extracted data for each event.
Postprocessing Call: After processing all events, it calls self.Postprocessing().
path
The path to the ROOT file(s).
tree
The name of the ROOT tree to read from.
self for method chaining.

sect2.title: Properties
sect2.para: These properties provide controlled access to the data managed by the SelectionTemplate.

listitem.para: @property PassedWeights: Retrieves a nested dictionary representing the weights that have passed the selection criteria. The C++ map is converted to a Python dictionary using as_basic_dict_dict.

@property GetMetaData: Retrieves a dictionary of Meta objects associated with the selected events. It iterates through self.ptr.matched_meta (a C++ map of strings to meta_t structs) and converts each meta_t into a Python Meta object.

sect1.title: C++ Interaction
sect1.para:
The SelectionTemplate class leverages Cython's capabilities for seamless C++ integration:
cdef class SelectionTemplate: Declares SelectionTemplate as a Cython extension type.
cdef selection_template *ptr: A C++ pointer to a selection_template object, managed by the Cython class.
from libcpp cimport string, map, pair, vector: Imports C++ standard library types.
from AnalysisG.core.tools cimport *: Imports C++ declarations from tools.pxd, including env, enc, as_basic_dict_dict, and tools().ends_with.
from AnalysisG.core.meta cimport *: Imports C++ declarations for meta_t and the Python Meta class.
from AnalysisG.core.io import IO: Imports the Python IO class for ROOT file handling.
pathlib, pickle: Python libraries used for path manipulation and object serialization.
